name: css
context: CSS styling: Tailwind CSS, CSS Modules, CSS-in-JS, responsive design, and animations
info: This guide covers styling frontend applications with modern CSS approaches. Tailwind CSS v4 is the recommended utility-first framework — it generates only the CSS you use and integrates with any component framework. CSS Modules provide scoped class names for component-level styles. CSS-in-JS libraries like styled-components and Emotion embed styles in JavaScript but add runtime cost. The guide also covers responsive design with media queries and container queries, Flexbox and Grid layout patterns, and CSS animations and transitions. All examples assume React components — see [[frontend/react]] for component patterns.

sections.title: Tailwind CSS
sections.body: Tailwind v4 uses CSS-first config with @theme directive. Utility classes map to CSS properties (e.g. `p-4` = padding 1rem, `flex` = display flex). Automatic content detection, built-in dark mode with `dark:` variant, container queries with `@container`. Generates only the CSS your markup uses. See [[frontend/bundling#vite]] for build integration.

sections.title: CSS Modules
sections.body: CSS Modules scope class names to the importing component, preventing global style conflicts. Import as `import styles from './Component.module.css'` and apply with `className={styles.className}`. Supported natively by Vite and Next.js. Use when you need traditional CSS with scoping, or when migrating from global CSS.

sections.title: Responsive design
sections.body: Mobile-first approach: default styles for mobile, then add breakpoint prefixes for larger screens (`sm:`, `md:`, `lg:`, `xl:`, `2xl:`). Container queries scope responsive rules to parent containers instead of viewport — better for reusable components. Use `clamp()` for fluid typography and spacing.

sections.title: Flexbox and Grid
sections.body: Flexbox for one-dimensional layouts: rows or columns, alignment, distribution, ordering. Grid for two-dimensional layouts: page structures, card grids, dashboard panels. Tailwind provides utility classes for both: `flex`, `grid`, `gap-*`, `grid-cols-*`, `items-center`, `justify-between`. Use `auto-fill`/`auto-fit` with `minmax()` for responsive grids without media queries.

sections.title: CSS animations
sections.body: CSS transitions for hover/focus state changes (`transition-colors`, `transition-transform`). Keyframe animations for complex sequences. Tailwind includes `animate-spin`, `animate-pulse`, `animate-bounce`, `animate-ping`. Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` with `motion-safe:` and `motion-reduce:` variants — see [[frontend/accessibility]] for accessibility considerations.
